title: The Complete Diffusion U-Net
concept: Combining convolutions, skip connections, timestep embeddings, and attention creates the standard diffusion denoising architecture. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Sampling: From Noise to Image.'

exercises.prompt: Which of these would help a network 'remember' fine details it saw early on, so it can use them again later?

options.text: A skip connection that carries early info forward
Making the image blurrier on purpose

exercises.explain: Skip connections carry detailed information from early layers straight across to later layers, so nothing important gets lost along the way.

cards.title: One Bridge, Four Superpowers
cards.body: Imagine a single machine that can zoom out to see the big picture, zoom back in for tiny details, remember 'how noisy' something is, and know which parts matter most — all at once. That machine is the diffusion U-Net, and today you'll see how all its pieces fit together!

cards.title: The Complete Diffusion U-Net
cards.body: The diffusion U-Net is the brain behind image-generating AI. It's built from four teammates working together: convolutions (that scan for patterns), skip connections (that keep details from getting lost), timestep embeddings (that tell the network how noisy the image currently is), and attention (that lets far-apart pixels talk to each other). Combined, they let the network guess and remove noise, step by step, until a clear image appears.

cards.title: Like a Renovation Crew
cards.body: Think of restoring an old, damaged photo. Convolutions are like workers scanning for scratches and patterns. Skip connections are sticky notes that save the original details before touching them, so nothing gets forgotten. Timestep embeddings are a clock on the wall telling everyone 'how far along' the restoration is. Attention is the foreman making sure workers on one side of the photo coordinate with workers on the other side. Together, the crew rebuilds the picture perfectly.

cards.title: Cleaning Up a Noisy Cat Photo
cards.body: A super noisy image goes into the U-Net. Convolutions shrink it down while spotting fur patterns and edges. The timestep embedding whispers 'this is step 800 of 1000, still very noisy!' Attention helps the network connect the cat's ears to its tail so proportions stay right. Then, going back up, skip connections hand back the sharp edge details that were saved earlier. Out comes a slightly less noisy, more cat-like image.

cards.title: Myth: More Parts Means It's Overcomplicated
cards.body: Myth: A network with four different components must be needlessly complex and confusing. Truth: Each piece has one clear job — convolutions find patterns, skip connections save details, timestep embeddings track noise level, and attention connects distant parts. Together they're simple teammates, not chaos, and each one solves a problem the others can't.

cards.title: Plain CNN vs. Full Diffusion U-Net
cards.body: A plain CNN just scans patterns layer by layer with no memory of noise level or long-distance connections. A diffusion U-Net adds skip connections, timestep info, and attention on top, making it far better suited for turning noise into images.

left.text: Only scans local patterns; no sense of noise level; loses fine details when shrinking the image

right.text: Scans patterns + keeps details (skip connections) + knows noise level (timestep embedding) + links distant pixels (attention)

exercises.prompt: Which part of the U-Net tells the network how noisy the current image is?
exercises.explain: The timestep embedding acts like a clock, telling the network exactly which noise step it's currently working on.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Skip connections help the U-Net keep fine details that would otherwise be lost when the image is shrunk down.
exercises.explain: True! Skip connections carry detailed information from the early 'shrinking' layers directly over to the later 'rebuilding' layers.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: In a diffusion U-Net, ___ allows far-apart parts of the image to share information with each other.
exercises.explain: Attention lets distant pixels 'talk' to each other, which helps keep shapes and proportions consistent across the whole image.

exercises.prompt: Which combination of parts makes up the full diffusion U-Net architecture?

options.text: Convolutions, skip connections, timestep embeddings, and attention
Just attention with no other parts needed

exercises.explain: All four teammates work together: convolutions spot patterns, skip connections save details, timestep embeddings track noise, and attention connects distant regions.

exercises.prompt: Boss Challenge: A student says, 'The diffusion U-Net just uses one trick to turn noise into images.' What's the best correction?

options.text: It actually combines four teammates: convolutions, skip connections, timestep embeddings, and attention, each solving a different problem
It only uses attention and nothing else
It only shrinks images and never rebuilds them
It uses random guessing with no structure at all

exercises.explain: Correct! The diffusion U-Net blends convolutions (pattern spotting), skip connections (detail saving), timestep embeddings (noise tracking), and attention (long-distance connections) into one powerful denoising machine. Next up: Sampling: From Noise to Image.
